mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate equity sell-offs and negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE spikes above the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving sustained corporate earnings revisions and positive quarterly equity returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both exceed 2-month moving average
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial production during late-cycle expansions signal supply chain bottlenecks and margin compression, causing immediate short-term equity drawdowns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases do not precede negative 5-day returns more often than random chance (win rate < 45%).

mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate combined with decelerating inflation indicates a successful soft landing policy mix, reducing real discount rates and generating positive monthly equity returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines while CPIAUCSL growth slows
failure_condition: The joint signal of easing rates and cooling inflation yields a negative or zero Rank IC for 20-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls reflect weakening economic activity and lower future corporate revenues, systematically depressing quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines show no statistically significant negative predictive power for 60-day forward returns (p-value > 0.10 in OLS regression).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 3.0% confirms robust aggregate demand, prompting immediate algorithmic buying and positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain flat or negative in over 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses the 3.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a confirmed recessionary regime signals collapsing corporate capacity utilization and earnings, driving negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during recessionary periods fail to predict negative 20-day returns with a win rate exceeding 55% over a 10-year backtest.
